release_review.summary: Browser Editor setup now keeps managed code-server transfers on the runtime-control loopback path reliable across reused connections and accepts safe archive-internal symlinks during code-server extraction.
checked_surfaces: Desktop runtime-control loopback proxy rewrites every eligible runtime-control request on reused keep-alive connections while leaving Local UI traffic on the original passthrough path
runtime-control WebSocket upgrade requests are rewritten only for the HTTP handshake and continue as the existing raw tunnel after upgrade
Browser Editor code-server archive extraction accepts safe internal relative symlinks required by current code-server packages
Archive extraction still rejects absolute symlinks, parent-escaping symlinks, hardlinks, symlink parents for regular file writes, and unsafe special entries
Desktop Browser Editor setup progress, provider link framing, session upload behavior, and Env App status polling keep their existing API contracts
Runtime Service protocol JSON fields, compatibility epoch, startup reports, sys.ping, Local UI health, and SSH runtime bootstrap keep the same compatibility window
release_review.same_window_rationale: This release changes Desktop-side request routing inside the loopback proxy and runtime-side code-server archive validation rules, but it does not add, remove, or rename Runtime Service JSON fields, runtime-control methods, startup report fields, sys.ping fields, Local UI health fields, provider-link framing, or SSH bootstrap contracts. Older and newer Desktop/Runtime pairs continue to use the same protocol window; the updated behavior only makes managed Browser Editor setup complete reliably with current code-server packages.
